text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 22 September 2014 

Dr Elaine Harper  
Riverside Health Centre  
48 Millbank, London SW1P 4QP  

Dear Dr Elaine Harper,                                                                                                         

Chloe Bennett, DOB: 22-01-1989, Hospital No: H472915 NHS No. 9382645170  
Flat 3, 27 Willow Road, London NW3 1TJ  

Epilepsy 

Medication: Patient reports current use of sodium valproate 500 mg twice daily since May 2014. She also takes folic acid 5 mg once daily. No other regular antiepileptic medication. 

Valproate Blood Level: 

15 September 2014: 58 mg/L (local reference 50–100 mg/L). Dose at time of sampling: 500 mg twice daily. 

Seizures: She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun both from being awake and asleep. She describes brief confusion afterwards lasting 10–20 minutes, with muscle soreness the following day. No tongue-biting recalled; occasional urinary incontinence has been reported. No consistent prodrome or warning noted. Triggers are uncertain; she wonders if poor sleep and alcohol the night before may have contributed to two of the recent events. 

History/Review: I conducted a face-to-face review today. She reports reliable adherence to valproate with only two missed doses in the last month. She has recently started a shift-based job which has disrupted her sleep pattern. No new neurological symptoms between events. Family history is negative for seizures. She does not drive and understands DVLA requirements. She keeps a diary and has arranged for a colleague to act as a witness at work if events occur on site. 

Investigations: MRI brain (March 2013) reported as normal. Routine EEG (April 2013) normal in wakefulness and drowsiness. Repeat EEG is planned given the recent increase in convulsive events. 

Plan: 
- Continue current valproate dose pending further review of adherence and lifestyle factors; we discussed sleep regularity, minimising alcohol, and medication timing as patient-reported strategies. 
- Arrange repeat EEG with sleep deprivation and book a follow-up in 8 weeks. 
- Check repeat valproate level, FBC, LFTs in 4 weeks or sooner if further clusters occur. 
- Safety advice reinforced: supervision near water, avoid heights/ladders, and use of a seizure alarm at night as discussed. 

I will update you once the EEG and blood results are available and will consider adjustments at that point in light of the observed pattern and patient preferences. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun
She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun both from being awake and asleep

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states monthly counts: 7 in September (so far), 4 in August, 2 in July, and 1 in June. Summing these: 7 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 14 seizures. The observation window spans June through September (four months, with September being partial). Therefore the frequency can be expressed as 14 per 4 months.
seizure_frequency_number: She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun
reference: She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun both from being awake and asleep
